frameworks.name: multivariate pattern decoding
frameworks.one_line: decoding models trained/tested across rules to probe representational structure

frameworks.name: category learning / prototype model
frameworks.one_line: category membership organized around a known prototype with typicality gradient

frameworks.name: rule-based cognitive control
frameworks.one_line: frontoparietal control modulates categorization rule (strict vs lax)

core_question: Do occipitotemporal category representations reorganize when generalization demands change rules?
key_assumptions: category membership is graded by typicality relative to a prototype
neural patterns can carry rule-dependent category information
strict vs lax rules impose different generalization tolerances
manipulations: categorization rule (strict vs lax) determining required generalization
stimulus typicality relative to a known category prototype
key_findings: frontoparietal regions tracked decisional demands across rules
occipitotemporal typicality representations differed between rules
cross-rule decoders failed to generalize despite within-rule success
interpretations: category representations in occipitotemporal cortex are shaped by abstract generalization demands, not stimuli alone
category structure is rule-flexible rather than fixed perceptually
